QF.id: Role of RTAs in Mutual Fund Transactions_14

question.text: What is the minimum contribution a sponsor must make to the net worth of an AMC?

answer.text: At least 26% of the AMC's net worth
At least 10% of the AMC's net worth
At least 40% of the AMC's net worth
At least 51% of the AMC's net worth

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI regulations require the sponsor to contribute at least 40% of the net worth of the AMC. This ensures the sponsor has significant skin in the game and is financially committed to the fund's success. **The Trap:** 51% sounds logical as it implies majority ownership, but the SEBI requirement is specifically 40%. The sponsor may hold more than 40%, but the minimum is 40%. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers sponsor eligibility criteria in detail. The 40% requirement works alongside the 5-year experience requirement and profitability criteria. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A sponsor's financial commitment through the 40% net worth contribution signals stability. When comparing AMCs for clients, a well-capitalized sponsor backing is a positive indicator of long-term sustainability.
